commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# shellfire -h
 usage: shellfire [-h] [-c [CONFIG]] [-d] [--generate PAYLOAD] [--version]
 
 An exploitation shell for command injection vulnerabilities.
 
 options:
 -h, --help          show this help message and exit
 -c [CONFIG]         load a named config on startup.
 -d                  enable debugging (show queries during execution)
 --generate PAYLOAD  generate a payload to stdout. PAYLOAD can be "php" or
                     "aspnet".
 --version           display version and exit.

apt_metadata.description:
exploiting LFI, RFI, and command injection vulnerabilities
This package contains an exploitation shell which focuses on exploiting LFI,
RFI, and command injection vulnerabilities.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use shellfire because ### shellfire.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `shellfire [-h] [-c [CONFIG]] [-d] [--generate PAYLOAD] [--version]` as documented upstream for shellfire.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair shellfire with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `shellfire --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
